_comment: Expected Cursor output for canonical/multi-event.json. Cursor split-event mapping: (1) before_tool_execute + ['shell','file_write'] splits into beforeShellExecution (for shell) and afterFileEdit (for file_write, closest Cursor equivalent — Cursor uses afterFileEdit not beforeFileEdit); (2) after_tool_execute + wildcard -> afterFileEdit (Cursor's only after event); (3) before_prompt -> beforeSubmitPrompt; (4) agent_stop -> stop. Cursor has limited event support so some semantics are approximate.
_warnings: Hook 1: array matcher split into separate Cursor events — 'shell' maps to beforeShellExecution, 'file_write' maps to afterFileEdit (Cursor has no beforeFileEdit event; afterFileEdit is the closest match but fires after, not before)
Hook 2: after_tool_execute wildcard mapped to afterFileEdit — Cursor only supports afterFileEdit for post-tool events, losing coverage of non-file tools
Hook 3: before_prompt blocking=true mapped to beforeSubmitPrompt — Cursor treats this as observe-only (blocking intent cannot be honored)
Hook 4: agent_stop blocking=false mapped to stop — Cursor treats this as observe-only
